title: Can't access postgres port 5432
body:
**Bug:**
- Can't access postgres port 5432

**Description:**
- Can't access postgres port 5432 when using podman but can when using docker

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `podman machine start`
2. `podman run --name db -e POSTGRES_USER=admin -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=Passw0rd -p 5432:5432 docker.io/library/postgres:13`
3. `PGPASSWORD=Passw0rd psql -h localhost -U admin`

**Results received:**
```
  psql: error: could not connect to server: Connection refused
          Is the server running on host "localhost" (::1) and accepting
          TCP/IP connections on port 5432?
  could not connect to server: Connection refused
          Is the server running on host "localhost" (127.0.0.1) and accepting
          TCP/IP connections on port 5432?
  could not connect to server: Connection refused
          Is the server running on host "localhost" (fe80::1) and accepting
          TCP/IP connections on port 5432?
```
**Results expected:**
`admin=#`

**Additional information:**
This is on `Mac OS X 10.15.7`. The above steps work in `Docker`, i.e. connection to database, via psql, is successful in docker and `admin=#` is returned.

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Client:
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.17
Built:        Mon Aug 30 15:15:26 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 16:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Please update your podman client to the latest version, this is already fixed

@Luap99 I'm on popos (ubuntu based) i have the version `3.4.4` which is the latest! 
how i can get the version 4?
